sentences.text: The doctor goes to the front desk to see if Connie arrived. He is waiting to see her.
Connie comes into the doctor's office. She pulls out a reorder and puts it on the desk to record.
The doctor let's Connie record but also invites John into the office to listen in to their conversation.
Connie takes off her jacket to get more comfortable and begins to tell a disturbing story. It's so disturbing that it really effects John. He puts his head down in dismay.
The end of story Connie is telling is so shocking and dramatic that the doctor falls out of his chair. Connie tries to catch him before he hits the floor.
Shea, a middle-aged man with blonde hair sits in a room. He's in a white sweater and wearing glasses and seems to be looking towards someone else.

The someone else is Christian, another middle-aged man, also wearing glasses, and he has dark hair and is dressed in a blue coat with a couple layers of clothing underneath. The room they are in appears to be a study.
The men are having a conversation with Shea speaking at the moment.
Christian replies and it seems to be a serious discussion.
The conversation continues.
Christian seems to receive a phone call.

In another part of the house, three people are moving from one room towards another. They are two women and a man. The man, Bradley, is the only one identified. He is wearing dark glasses. The women are well-dressed.
In the study Shea and Christian continue their conversation.
Christian gets up and greets the new arrivals at a doorway.
Denzel comes in to the thrift store with his friend. His friend felt that he had been ripped off in something he bought from the store. Denzel is acting as his friend's bulwark.
They first talk to the guy at the cash register. He disagrees with Denzel's friend.
Denzel's friend continues to lay out his position on how he felt ripped off.
Mila chimes in from her job stacking books and just generally working. Mila states that she thinks she should go and get Tom who is the manager.
Both Tom the manager and Gary the owner of the used book and thrift store come out and basically tell Denzel's friend that they will compensate him for how he feels wronged.
James frantically picks up the phone. "Hello!" he screams into the telephone. All he hears is a deafening silence. There was no dial tone, James was on his own.

James peeks out go his window to see that the lights across the courtyard, in the apartment across from his has gone out. "Where did he go?" James whispered to himself. What he had seen in that window moments before frightened him like he's never been before.
With the telephone still in his hands, James had to think fast.
"That's it. I'll go next door." James heads for the door.
As James heads for the door, he hears footsteps. The footsteps stop and the knob begins to turn.
Bound to his wheelchair, James quickly rolls himself into the bedroom and shuts the door.
No where to go, no one to help. James stares at his bedroom door. The footsteps are now heard from within his apartment. His heart pounds as the door slowly begins to open.
Jennifer enters her husband Gary's study. He is sitting in the dark and has been drinking a bit. He knows what is coming.
Jennifer confronts Gary on his serial infidelity. She is fed up after years of Gary's behavior. She unloads on him in a very final and emotional manner.
Jennifer has now let out all of her feelings from years of Gary's emotional abuse. She has now reached an inner calmness. She also feels a deep sadness.
Jennifer moves in closer to Gary as she feels more sure of herself.

Jennifer closes in even more and states that she can't believe how they have ended up in this situation. She expresses her deep disappointment and sadness. She states that she is leaving for good and that Gary will never see her again.

Gary smirks a bit. He is a total bastard and hasn't really felt anything for Jennifer for years. He just kind of smirks and scoffs at Jennifer's outburst and then just shrugs as she leaves Gary's study.

Vincent, the ballet teacher, looks on as Benjamin and Natalie practice a routine together. Their stances show that they are feeling uncomfortable about the scrutiny they are being put on. Vincent isn't happy with the performance.
Natalie's face is filled with pain as she tries to get her emotions in check for the routine. Benjamin looks on with a hopeful look on his face, thinking the routine will go better the next time.
Vincent looks on, still indifferent. He is not impressed with their abilities and think they should be doing better. He expects better from them.
Natalie looks at Vincent with concern, wondering if he has some sort of hate for her personally. She doesn't think his critique is being fair.

Later, Natalie goes back to the dressing room where one of her classmates, Ksenia, addresses her. She accuses her of not taking the routine seriously. She tells her that she needs to get her head in the game.
A man dressed in a button down shirt and tie, fixes their tie in front of a mirror near a dresser in their bedroom.
A woman sleeps in the nude, covered by a sheet, on a bed in a messy bedroom, behind the man.
The man looks up into the mirror, at the woman behind him. The woman sits up and gazes at the man. They both look surprised.
The man walks over to the woman, who is sitting up in the bed, covered only by a sheet. The man reaches into their front pocket.
The man returns again to the woman, standing next to her seated on the bed. They are now fully dressed in a suit, to go out.
Kelly saw the entrance sign to the diner. She was very nervous. She had an important meeting in this diner.
The walked with her friend Sue. They met up for the first time in five years. Kelly had a huge favor to ask of Sue.

Kelly finally told Sue that she wanted her husband gone, and it didn't matter how it got done. Kelly knew that Sue had ties to the underworld. SHe knew that Sue would be the one to make sure it was quick, painless and untraceable.
Sue was delighted that Kelly would ask her. Sue hated Kelly's husband and longed for the day when he was no longer in the picture. Sue would get her best friend back. They would talk everyday again.
They sat at the table for hours plotting. They enjoyed their time together coming up with the perfect murder plot.
Jack stares into the bathroom, a quizzical expression on his face.
The hotel bathroom, painted green, appears empty. A shower curtain obscures half of the bathtub.
Astonishingly, a naked woman suddenly appears to push back the shower curtain and steps out of the tub.
The woman embraces jack, who awkwardly returns the gesture, but then is horrified to discover that her form has transformed, becoming old and muddy.
The newly transformed old crone looms toward Jack.
A terrified and confused Jack spreads his arms wide, hoping to halt the woman's advance.
Rosario stands at the edge of the abyss looking out over the edge surrounded by a bank of lights as she tries to determine the next step.
Meanwhile Anthony stands nearby viewing the computer screens that he knows will help them achieve their goal.
As he works, he gives instructions to Rosario to help her know the next move, but he's also giving himself a pep talk to keep his head in the game.
Eventually Anthony reaches for the vial that Rosario needs to send carefully down the abyss to safety for those waiting at the bottom.
The bank of lights that surrounds the chamber silently looks on while they work together to accomplish their goal.

Jim was on his deathbed wondering if every evil thing he'd done in life was leading up to this moment. He'd stolen money from the weak and innocent to build his billion dollar fortune. He was contemplating whether a deathbed confessional would save his soul. He was also wondering where his family was.
His lawyer was actually meeting up with his family downtown.
They were talking about dividing Jim's fortune. They were just as greedy as Jim was. They couldn't even wait for him to pass away before they could talk about how the money would be divided.
Jim's wife was with his brother. THey were planning on getting married right after Jim's funeral. The secret relationship would no longer be in the shadows.
THey all agreed that once Jim was gone, they'd enjoy their money in seperate countries and never return to America again. They knew the backlash they would get from the wedding would be severe.
A small dog is lying sick on a wood floor. There are little bits of food around him. He has gotten into some human food and couldn't resist himself.

Ben walks into to the room with the sick dog and is horrified since he knows it is Cameron's favorite pet. He at first doesn't really know what to do and panics a bit. He thinks about the fact that Cameron will think he is responsible for the dog's state.
Ben then grabs the dog up and gets ready to try to revive it, but it instead attacks him. The dog was just faking illness.
Ben tosses the dog away and then prepares to fight him.
Ben and the dog square off and Ben wonders where this is going to lead and how it will effect his relationship with Cameron.

As Cameron walks into the room, the little dog lunges at Ben's groin. Cameron freaks out. This is about as badly as this could have gone. Cameron recovers the dog and tells Ben that maybe he should go home. Maybe they should take a break.
The stern sheriff, who is wearing a hat and sporting a mustache, is frowning at the unfolding scene.
He sees a man holding a guy while two other men have their hands up in the air. A woman looks on, startled.
The man hoists his gun and threatens to shoot the sheriff.
The two men and the woman flee, as the man attempts to reload his gun.
The sheriff shoots the man through the bars of the building before he can do so, killing him.
Jim downs a drink while Jeff watches also feeling thirsty.
Karen looks at them in utter disgust over how they are behaving. She tries to tell them that they should behave more civilized.
Jim looks at Jeff as if to say they don't need to take this kind of crap, and Jeff shrugs in agreeance.
Jim then leans in to try and kiss Jeff hoping that they will finally end up together.
Jeff denies him the kiss and Jim leans against the bar defeated and sad.
Tim got the important email on his laptop. He just wish he knew what it meant.

Tim thought for hours about what this weird phrase meant. He knew it had something to do with the bank robbery that he and his associates were planning. He couldn't simply ask what it meant in the email. That would be evidence that could be used in court.
Tim began having second thoughts about the robbery. What if something went wrong? He wasn't cut out for prison.
He decided he would call off the robbery just as he heard footsteps coming up to his office. He knew that this could only be Sam coming up to talk about the plan.

Sam walked through the door with terrible news. Something had gone wrong already. Another member of the crew got cold feet about the robbery and killed most of the people involved. Only Sam and Tim were left. They needed to leave town before they were victims as well.
A man is in his car, gritting his teeth in anger.
He furtively looks out the car window and sees two people approaching his car.
He gets out his car to confront them.
His girlfriend looks at him in disappointment and asks him if he thinks that he can get away with what he did, while the sheriff looks on.
He tells her that he never meant for any of this to happy, and that he hopes she will understand one day why he did what he did.

Jeff and Jim sit on a bed wearing matching white robes. Both look tired but alert as they turn toward something across the room. The setting looks like a hotel or a shared apartment, with curtains drawn behind them. They seem to be planning or thinking about what to do next after waking up.

Jeff and Jim sit close together, both wearing robes as they read a newspaper. They study the page carefully, leaning in with curious and serious looks. It seems like they’ve come across an article that catches their attention and might give them a new idea or plan.

A newspaper is open to an article about a fancy gala happening soon. The page shows a photo of two people dressed nicely beside the story. The headline talks about the city’s elite being expected at the event.

Jeff and Jim sit side by side in the barbershop, each wearing a blue cape. The barber stands beside them, cutting hair while they smile and laugh. The mood is light and friendly, showing them enjoying the moment together.

Jeff and Jim sit in a barbershop wearing blue capes while two stylists work on their hair. Jeff looks calm as his hair is dried, and Jim reads a magazine with a small smile. The scene feels relaxed and happy, showing them both enjoying their time there.
George is wearing a ski mask and threatening the bank workers with a gun.
Robert complies with George's instructions to put his hands behind his head. Susan, crouched beside him, has her hands on her head.
George tells the kneeling bank workers not to move if they value their lives.
George goes to the vault and empties as much cash as he can get into a bag.
Val, also wearing a ski mask and hoisting a gun trained on the bank workers, tells George to hurry it up.

The sky is blue and calm, with the leaves of the trees steady and unmoving, just within her view as she gently runs he hands along a fresly hung sheet on a makeshift clothesline, the warmth of the sun baskig agaist her and the fabric, casting a slight shadow and aiding in drying out te fabric. Its' soft smooth surface felt against her fingertips as she moves them along its' surface.

In the distance, near the house where they stay, its' surface made of a mixture of gray and white stone, her husband looks intensily at a log as he swings with all his might to split it, the clinging of his shirt ad the sweat on his forhead showcasing the intesnity of the labor he has endured.

Hearing him,she rushes to the fronnt of the house, standing at its' doorstep, a surface made of carefully layed, even cut stones, the creeping in of fog seen just behind her, its' embrace enveloping the mountains and hills, making them slowly fade into a whitish-gray haze, the beauty of the treeline ad the green grass cared for by nature still seen.

Though he is some distace away, the echo off her footsteps and perhaps the sense of her presenncce draws his eyes toward her. He looks on, with a mixture of admiration or concern, the well of which they rely on for their daily fresh water a constant reminder of their distance from the world.

In an effort to take it all in, he turns his face away from hers and slowly closes his eyes, in deep thought about the task that still need to be done and the struggles that they must endure, but also toward the idea that, even in this momeent, even in the bastion of hard work, their togetherness still allows a calm to rush over him.

The distace between them seems far in this instance, showing a seperation betweeen task that must be completed, her eyes now fixated on the well, dropping the pail within her hands into it, retreiving the fresh water from deep underground.

Though he attempts to remain calm and still his worries, work helping to some extent, they creep in anyway. The two of them seeing each other and interacting everyday, but the emotions he feels hidden from her, so as to prevvent her happiness from fading. They overwhelm hi in this moment, his eyes close, attempting to stem the tide of guilt and sadness that is welling up inside him.

Seemingly unaware of her husbands internal struggles, she rushes forward toward the house ad away from the well, her pail shifting to one side as she plants one foot after the other steep into the surface of the soft soil, its' fullness giving way to her weight. The fog liftig above the hills behind her, a grayish blue haze filling the sky.
Three children sit at the wreckage of a burnt down house.
Mel, Heath, and some others alongside their horse-drawn carriage approach the house.
Mel, with blood all over his shirt, stares at the ruins and a dead child in front of it, blood on his chest.
Mel looks down at the child.
He sees the child's bloodied hand and miniature figurines of horses beside him.
Mel looks down with his eyes closed and a deep frown.
Heath looks ahead, taking in the scene with a cold, steely face.
The survivors gather near a picket fence and some trees to pray.
The blue football team kicks the ball off to start the game.
Number 14 is a bit nervous because he can see on the kickoff the red team players seem extra physical. He does not want to get hurt.
Rene, the coach of the red team, watches the game menacingly as his old rival is the coach of the other team. Roger, the assistant coach, instructs players to do whatever to get the win.
The red team members play very aggressively because they want to please their coach. They know they can get kicked off the team if they don't do well.
As the game winds up, two red team players taunt blue team members. Blue teams members at this point have given up and are losing pretty bad.

Two women and a man are standing outside in front of a tall, unique tree or garden decoration. The women are wearing long elegant gowns with shawls, and the man is dressed in a suit with a bow-tie. It looks like they are at a special event or party in the evening.

Five men are standing close together indoors. They are all wearing suits and ties, looking happy and proud. Two of the men have their hands shown and the others don't. They seem to be posing for a formal photo, maybe at a meeting or celebration.

Back outside, A different group of women are grouped together wearing fancy dresses and large hats. They look like they are enjoying a party or gathering. In front of them, an older couple are sitting and talking.In another frame, on the right side, a man stands confidently, maybe giving a speech or greeting guests.

People are gathered outside near a concrete building. The men and women are dressed in nice clothes, standing and sitting together. Most of them seem to be focused on the man in the black suit standing in the middle.
A man is standing in a small room or office with drawings and photos pinned to the wall. He is smiling while holding a paper and presenting his work.

A woman sits on a leather coach in a dark room with her hands folded on her lap. She is wearing a pink robe. She appears to be sitting at a coffee table in front of her with two vases sitting on it. She appears to be upset.
The camera zooms in on the woman's face. She is upset about recent events in her life and the totality of where her life has been. She stares blankly into the distance. Her hair is mussed.
Here eyes are heavy. She tired, but also at her breaking point. Her emotions have been weighing heavy on her.

The woman stands up and screams, whatever she has upset about or thinking about has caused an emotional outburst. Her mouth is fully open and the muscles in her neck are tight. Her robe has open but she nothing is exposed.

After the outburst, the woman decides to rest. The woman moves to the bedroom and lays horizontally across the bed. She falls closes her eye and starts to fall asleep. The room starts to fill with smoke as she lies there.
Brian is concerned about the developments surrounding the operation that he is supervising.
Joan makes a call, somewhat nonchalantly while she examines her nail job, hoping to assuage Brian's concerns.
The call isn't turning out as well as Joan was hoping as she looks on with some concern regarding the conversation.
Meanwhile, Julia stands in the window under the eye of someone watching through a rifle scope.
Matt is the one watching, and is also the one on the phone with Joan which is what has caused Joan such concern finding out that Matt can see them through the window.
Tom, who is in another room from Brian, Joan and Julia, examines some documents and directs research to try and find Matt.
Brian, Joan and Julia continue to increase in anxiety as the conversation with Matt continues.
Julia becomes resigned to their possible fate as she closes her eyes resignedly.
Joan looks through the window somewhat fearfully as she looks to where she believes Matt has been watching them through the window.
Amy is parked in a car in front of a house in the suburbs. Her window is rolled down and she is looking across the street.
Christian is standing between two hedges in front of a house. His right hand is held up against his chest.
Amy, wearing a raincoat, gets out of the car and rushes out of the car. She has a worried look on her face.
Amy rushes to help Christian, who is about to collapse onto the sidewalk. Christian is mumbling something to Amy.
Amy gets Christian into her car, seating him on the driver's side.

There is a large group of people sitting inside a fancy restaurant or dining room, having dinner at tables. The walls are covered in dark red wallpaper with a floral design, and there are two lit wall lamps. A big painting hangs in the middle of the back wall, with green decorations or plants on each side. The people are dressed nicely in suits and dresses. The tables have candles and wine glasses on them. Everyone is chatting while they eat their meals.

A man, James, is wearing a dark blue suit sits at a bar and looks over his shoulder at something we can’t see. Behind him, is the same dark red patterned wallpaper, and small lamps that give off a warm light. Two other people are at the bar, talking to a bartender who is wearing a white shirt. A shiny silver pitcher sits on the counter. The lighting is soft and dim, making the room feel quiet and serious. The man looks focused as he turns his head.

This scene now shows what James appears to be looking at. Through a doorway, he can see a busy dining room with many people sitting at tables and having dinner. A blonde-haired woman, Kim, in a bright green clothing is in the middle of the room, which makes her stand out. A waiter is serving food at one of the tables while other guests are chatting and eating.

Kim is now standing near a doorway, turning her head to look inside the room. Her hair is neatly styled in a bun. A man in a gray suit and bow tie walks toward her with a slight smile. The background is busy, but the moment centers on the woman and her slightly surprised reaction.

A light-colored car is parked on the side of a city street. A man sits inside the car, looking ahead through the windshield. Behind the car, there is a large brown building with tall windows, and farther back, a tall gray tower rises into the sky. Two other men are walking on the sidewalk near the front of the car, wearing a dark suit and hat. The sky is clear, and the scene looks calm.

Three men sit together in a small diner, talking quietly over coffee. They lean toward each other as if discussing something serious. The mood feels tense but controlled, and each man listens carefully while trying not to draw attention.

A man in a winter coat stands at a counter, speaking to someone across from him. He looks uneasy, holding a piece of paper while the person listens. The light from the window makes the scene feel cold and quiet.

Outside the diner, two men stand talking near the door. One wears a red coat while the other keeps his hands in his pockets against the cold. Their breath shows in the winter air as they speak quietly, looking uneasy.
The man in the tan coat stands near the road, his face tense as he listens to the other. Snow covers the ground, and cars pass in the distance. The cold wind makes the scene feel lonely and still.

The two men stand by the roadside, their faces tense as they talk. The man in red looks frustrated while the other listens quietly. Snow covers the ground, and cars pass slowly in the distance, adding to the cold, uneasy feeling of the moment.
In order to cure some of the boredom he decided to play with a dradle. He spins it over and over again wishing he were somewhere else.
It seems like the church service is quite a bore. If you're not distracted by something else, then you are sleeping.
There were some people who were eagerly listening as the preacher was delivering his message. There were some points he made that peeked some interests.
The reader was nervous as he was reading scripture to the congregation. It was his first time at this church.
The boys mother looked back at him as he sat down once he was finished reading. Everyone near him looked very down and somber.
Jason and Dexter look down at the victim in a crime scene. Dexter puts on a pair of gloves to handle some evidence.
Nick stands outside of the room and curiously looks on. He knows nothing can be done.
Jason and Dexter talk to Nick in an attempt to figure out what happened.
One of the men who committed the crime rolled up his black ski mask and called his partner.
His partner, Jason, talks to him on the phone about how they plan to get away with the perfect crime.
A bar, old and worn, but home to many regular drinkers.
Jason, a regular, slams his empty drink on the bar, demanding another from the bartender.
The bartender does not respond, as Nick seems very drunk at the moment.
However, he fills Jasons glass eventually, not wanting to cause any trouble with nick.
However, of course, his mistake comes back to haunt him, as after this drink is done, Nick decides to get into an argument with Jason, and the two start a fight, with Jason shoving nick.
Jason then grabs a nearby drink and shoves it into his face, starting a fight through the entire bar. It quickly became mayhem and chaos.

Father Joe appeared amidst the chaos with two off-duty soldiers. He proclaimed that a very special event was about to take place. He raised his hands and stood in front of a makeshift tabernacle. Explosions rocked the land behind him.
Just then a chopper descended from the sky. Instead of artillery and ammunition, it lowered a young calf.
The soldiers looked on in disbelief. A cow? How is that going to help us in the fight? The sun set and a sense of calm and confusion washed over the battlefield.

Martin clapped and smiled. He was the only one that knew what was going on. This was a ceremonial feast the villagers used to honor their ancestors. But now a feast to boost the morale of the soldiers.
Frederic rubbed his hands together expecting a good meal. Martin assured him that it would be the best he's ever had.

Laurence and Albert weren't so sure. They were vegetarians and couldn't eat anything being offered. Instead they grabbed a six pack of Budweiser and pulled out some playing cards. Something to pass the time.
Martin cracked open a Budweiser too. This was a celebration after all. Meanwhile the cow was made into a soup and ready.

The ceremonial soup was passed around along with plans for tomorrow's battle. The men laughed as they sampled the soup while going over battle plans. They tried to guess the ingredients while strategizing for war.

As the men savored the soup, laughter rang out across the camp. The men temporarily forgot about the war. The strangeness and warmth that day brought to the battlefield would make it memorable, not because of its grandeur.
Ben comes over to pick Cameron up for a date. He notices a dog on the couch who seems agitated by his presence.
Cameron and Lin, her roommate, tell Ben to stay away from the little dog. He might bite if Ben tries to pet him.
The ladies were right. The dog does bite Ben when he attempts to pet him.
He falls to the floor and says he is in pain. He threatens to call the pound and report the dog as a dangerous animal.
Lin gets angry and tells Ben to get out of her apartment. She says he deserved to be bitten since he petted the dog even after he was warned not to do so. Ben leaves in a huff.
Robert and his partner were looking for a criminal who had just robbed a local saloon. They wandered the streets and went into any building with an unlocked door.
The wounded robber was hiding out in an abandoned building while keeping a close eye on any intruders.
Robert and his crew found the thief, who was armed, and a shootout ensued.
The robber stayed low and took aim, but didn't last very long.
Robert and his partner stood over the defeated robber and congratulated each other on the capture.
Philip stands in an open booth, listening to something on his headphones while Jude look towards him from outside the booth.
Matt enters the room and greets the pair.
Matt comes closer and smiles.
Matt approaches the booth, now closed, putting his hand on the glass and looking at the pair with a worried look on his face.
Matt tries to persuade them to listen to him.
Jude is now also listening in on the headphone alongside Philip, and both are staring at Matt with a look of suspicion.
Matt stares back at them with a look of worry.
Matt is reading a letter while walking with Jude.
Matt starts laughing, because something in the letter makes him happy. His father has died, and he will receive a large inheritance as a result.
Jude is a bit disturbed by Matt's lack of sadness upon learning of his father's death. He thinks it's odd that Matt only seems to be interested in the money he'll now receive.
Matt tells Jude that his father never seemed interested in his life, and that is why he is not sad about his death. He also tells Jude that his father abused him when he was a child.

Matt asks Jude to have lunch with him to celebrate his windfall. Jude makes up an excuse so he can avoid having lunch with Matt. He cannot shake the feeling that Matt is not a good person and cannot be trusted.

There’s a big room where a bunch of people are having a fancy dinner. The tables are long and full of food and shiny cups. At the end of the room, There are another set of people sitting higher up. The walls have lights that make everything glow. You can see the whole thing through some black bars, like looking through a window.

A man named Eric is sitting at a fancy table covered with a patterned cloth. He’s wearing a red hat with gold decorations and furry clothing. In front of him are shiny silver dishes, a tall cup, and plates of food like fruit and grapes. Other people are eating and talking at the long table.

Eric’s face is shown from the side, focusing on his left profile. He’s still wearing his red hat and fancy clothes with fur. The lighting from the room hits his face, showing that he looks slightly amused.

Mark and David are sitting at a table covered with a patterned cloth. A bunch of grapes inside of a silver cup sits in the middle between them. David, on the left, wears a fur-trimmed coat and a cap, looking over to the right. Mark, on the right, has a beard and has somewhat of a surprised look on his face.

Many people in elegant clothes fill a big room. A lady with a fancy hat sits in the center. A long table holds food like roasted chickens and fruit. Sunlight shines through tall windows, making everything glow.

Gerard picks Jennifer up in a bicycle rickshaw on the boardwalk in Atlantic City. He seems to be in a good mood and tries to strike up a conversation but she is in a rush, ignoring him, and looking through her purse for something.
The rickshaw pulls up outside of the Trump Taj Mahal casino with a crowd of people milling in front of it.
Jennifer looks down into her purse, apparently looking for money to pay for the ride, still ignoring Gerard. She pays, says thanks and gets off his bike.
Gerard is stunned by her behavior - and lack of a tip. But he is also intrigued by her.
Gerard leaves the casino entrance and heads back down the boardwalk for another fare.
A man with a beard, George, sits behind the the wheel of his car, which is parked on a street in front of a house. He watches something intently.

Kevin and his partner Dan sit in their car, Kevin behind the wheel, Dan in the back seat. They're watching George. They've been following him for some time. The two of them chat while they're waiting.
George pulls away and Kevin drives foward, past the house where George was parked
Kevin drives slowly at first, trying not to alert George.
Unfortunately, George sees them and rushes through an intersection, trying to get away from them.
Kevin tries to follow George, but William jumps in front of his car, stopping him.
The room is filled with those who have been injured and the people tending to them. Worried looks cover most of their faces.
Jude lays in a bed sweating profusely, a bad wound to his neck is evident and has been poorly dressed. The pain of his injury is evident on his face as he struggles to breathe.
Nicole walks into the room full of injured men and spots Jude in his bed. A look of sorrow crosses her face because she realizes the seriousness of his injury.
Nicole leads down to talk to Jude and looks concerned. Jude sees her and briefly smiles through his anguish.
A look of despair crosses Nicole's face. She was unable to hide her emotions upon seeing Jude in his condition.
Ellen knew it wasn't her turn to do the laundry. One of these other deadbeats around the house had to do it.
Robert hadn't done the laundry since their anniversary, months ago. He thought it wasn't man's work and beneath him.
Ellen grabbed a baseball bat they kept by the door in case of intruders.
Leonardo had seen this before. Practically once a week his parents would come to blows.
Robert dared Ellen to hit him.
Leonardo took up Ellen's side, screaming that he'd pick up the bat next.
Robert just kept antagonizing them.
Before things could get any worse, they heard the doorbell ring. The pizza was here.
Sam is walking through tall grass in the woods when he stumbles across a fallen elf with large antlers and kneels down to take a photograph of it.
Richard and Brooke are also walking through the same woods together, side by side. As they weave their way through the dense trees, they talk about many things.
On the beach, Linda is playing in the water with her older brother and their dog. Her brother is swinging around a large rubber tube.
Linda's older brother lifts her up onto the air over his head, playfully.
Linda, now soaking wet, turns toward their dog.
Linda's brother runs through the water holding a ball, with the dog right behind him. They enjoy their play.
Angelina steps into the bedroom shocked to see what is on the computer screen.
She runs to the computer to read the messages that had popped up as Jonny, Renoly, and Matthew stand by.
Matthew jokes as Angelina explains that they were hacked and now have to pay a ransom to a hacker.
Johnny calms Angelina down from her hysterics of the situation because she is concerned with how they are going to fix this. Matthew looks on as an idea pops into his head.
Meanwhile, the hacker named Fisher stands by and waits for a message back.
Fisher sees their reply and laughs to himself amused as they send him the money for the ransom.
Eddie enjoyed looking up at the clouds. It was fun to imagine what objects the shape of the clouds resembled.

Kelly didn't like gazing at the clouds as much as her brother, but she played along to make him happy. What Kelly and Eddie did not know was that a terrifying event would soon disturb their care-free world.
That night, Kelly and Eddie's father, Jimmy, was in a serious car accident. An ambulance took Jimmy to the local hospital.
Sharon, Jimmy's wife, raced to the hospital as soon as she heard of the accident, taking the kids with her.
Jimmy was overcome with emotion when he saw his family, but everyone was relieved to hear that his injuries were not life-threatening.
Harrison, a middle aged-man is in a jungle-like area. He is in outdoor clothing and sporting a hat. He appears to be on the move.
He gets to the edge of a waterway and swings on a vine towards the water.
He is now in the water and his hat can be seen, but not his face.
Now his face is seen as he struggles to swim.
On a bank are natives armed with bows. It is likely that they are threatening Harrison.
Now there is a seaplane on the water. It difficult to see, but it looks as if Harrison is clinging to it in an attempt to be rescued. In the foreground we get a glimpse of the threatening natives.

Daniel sits at a table in a dark room. The light hits his face as he looks around with a serious expression. He seems calm, but there’s tension in the air, like he’s thinking about something important.

The group around him looks uneasy. One man leans forward, speaking with force while the others watch. Daniel listens closely, his face still serious as he tries to keep the situation from getting worse.

Daniel stands at the front of the room, his head tilted slightly as he speaks. He looks confident but serious, and the men behind him seem to respect his words. The light catches his face, showing a mix of calm and control.
Another man steps forward, looking upset as he talks. His hands move sharply while others around him watch in silence. The tension grows, and the air feels thick with anger and pride.

Daniel gestures with open hands, trying to calm everyone down. His voice seems steady, and his eyes stay locked on the man across from him. The crowd watches closely, waiting to see what happens next.

Gary looks at Daniel with a steady gaze, trying to read his expression. The room stays quiet as both men seem to understand the weight of the moment. Daniel lowers his hands, and the tension slowly fades while everyone waits for what comes next.

Reese was in it to win it. Win what? Didn't matter. As long as there were people around to beat, she was determined to come out on top. In this case, the setting was high school. History class. Senior year.

Matthew was a young teacher but had been around enough to know Reese's type. Yeah, she knew her stuff but damn was she annoying. It didn't help that he found himself attracted to her. This was a problem, obviously, and he hated himself for even having those thoughts. He would have called on anyone else but nobody even bothered to raise their hands; they knew Reese would be first.
Reese gave the answer. It was correct, of course. She was always correct. It gave her a bit of an attitude, knowing she could do no wrong.

Years later, as an accomplished attorney, she decided to run for office. Her opposition decided to do some research on her to see if there was any dirt they could dig up. Naturally, they looked through her yearbook.

They found nothing until the very last page. A photograph of the yearbook staff, forgotten for years but now brought into the light once more. Reese is front and center, of course. But surrounding her in the photo are four people with plaid shirts. They look normal enough, but it would later come out that they were running an extortion ring at the high school. Reese was the bait. They'd frame teachers and keep quiet in exchange for better grades, good recommendations, and occasionally money. Reese had managed to keep her name clear, but this photo was enough for the opposing campaign to use against her. Guilt by association.
While at the park, Jake walks up to Greta. Greta takes time from her phone call to talk to Jake.

Jake asks Greta if she has heard from Natalie because Jake wanted to tell her about Ashton looking for her. Jake also explains that Ashton was nervous about talking to Natalie because he was going to propose to her at the park.
Greta lets Jake know that she knows exactly where Natalie is.
Natalie talks to a friend in the distance about how the tables needed to be set up.
Ashton, who had been wandering all over the park for Natalie, finally sees Natalie at the table. Ashton waits for Natalie to stop talking to walk up to her.
When Natalie is finally alone, Ashton starts to walk toward her, but ends up taking the ring out and have second thoughts.
A masked and armed man, Val, is scoping out part of a building.
Val quickly glances down at an office filled with workers, desks, and computers. He then quickly turns his head away from the office so the workers won't be able to see that he is masked.
A frightened office worker rubs his eyes while whispering to a fellow worker while trying to conceal a cell phone.
Robert and the other workers have been told to put their hands on their heads. Robert is relatively calm for the situation as he tries to figure out what to do.
Val has gained access to the safe and is unlocking the security boxes.
Val continues robbing the safe.
The bank robbery had gone badly. Robert, a career criminal, and Val, the wounded accomplice he carried on his shoulder, had emerged from the bank to find swarms of police officers outside.
Al was a veteran detective. He had been pursuing Robert and his gang for months. He now plunged into a furious firefight with the criminals.
Robert, with Val in tow, continued to desperately search for a way out of the police ambush.
Robert saw a weak point in the perimeter created by the police. He rushed forward with his rifle blazing.
One of Robert's rounds struck a police officer. The man fell, seriously injured.
Colin and Jamie show up at a bar to talk business with John. They have been investigating the death of a local high school student who OD'd on drugs. Several of John's associates surround them.
John invites Jamie and Collin to sit down. They oblige him and take a seat.

Colin and Jamie tell John they know he's selling drugs, and one of those drugs he sells just killed a kid in high school. John laughs and tells them both that they don't know what kind of trouble they will be in if they don't look the other way.
Colin tells John "we can't be intimidated" and that John "will be going down." All they need is time.
Jamie tells John he "knows what kind of scum bag he is and that this will be the highlight of his career." Getting trash of the street is his job.
" We finally have him' Christoff thought to himself as he approached the guard at the door. 'What is he doing?" he asked. "Just waiting" said the guard.
Kristoff entered the room and saw the prisoner in a hood. "And we finally have you" Christoff said. "It looks like it" said the prisoner, Brad.
"You should have told your people to back off" said Christoff in the dark room with only himself and Brad. And what good would that have done asked the Brad.
"Maybe we would have been kinder to you. Christoff spat out. "I would never have expected it, and don't need it." Shouted Brad.
'You will wish you had backed off" Christoff remarked. "This was the outcome the whole time." Brad replied as Christoff removed his gun from his holster.

Ominous clouds rolled in as a news reporter began taping for a nightly broadcast. The cameraman frames the image with a church surrounded by barbed wire fencing. This was no ordinary church. It was the church on the grounds of a county prison. A place of newfound infamy.

This was the church where the infamous Charles Manson practiced while in prison. He was known for the grisly late 60s murders but later found religion while incarcerated. But the church was now known for something far more sinister.
Two detectives rummage through torn clothing. Some covered in blood. All found scattered around a church within a prison. They could not believe what they were seeing.

Inside the church they found an even more gruesome scene. Four policemen were hanging in the basement. All suffering the same wounds. All were found with one thing in their coat pockets: A single car key.

Just outside the prison, a man in a leather jacket driving a red convertible is about to exit the gates. He pulls out a keyring filled with keys. All are there except four. He sees another prison guard just as he's about to leave. He grins, hiding his bloodstained key ring just out of sight. Somehow he got away with it. He smirked and sped off.
The blonde woman was determined to get her old boyfriend back. He was a rich man and had become even richer in the past few years.
But her old boyfriend was with a new woman. Even though he was blind, he could see through his ex-girlfriend's ways.
The blonde woman tried telling the new girlfriend about their past in order to make her feel uncomfortable.
They all filed into their private dining room to have dinner after sipping drinks at the bar.
This was not over and the blonde woman would not give up until she got what she wanted.
The blonde woman wanted to close a deal today.
It was very important that she locked in her investors today so that she could continue funding her schemes.

The blonde woman told some lies about how successful her business had been so far. She told her potential investors that they had a lot of competition and that she had to choose between all her potential investors.
The other woman was amused but did not believe the blonde woman.
The brunette woman advised her husband to steer clear as she thought this was a scam.
The employee looked down below as he saw pirates come aboard the ship.
The leader of the pirates voiced his demands.
The owner of the ship did not think that those pirates had any leverage on him. He had a smug look on his face and laughed at them.
Soon, it was clear that his own employees were working with the pirates. His employees turn their guns on him. The owner quickly realized this was serious.
They took his daughter and threatened her but the boat owner did not care. He was a selfish man, and would do anything to keep his power even if it meant sacrificing his own daughter.
Shia had only worked for the Thompson Brothers Law Firm for a few days and was just getting used to the job when the managing partner, Billy Thompson entered his office and extended his hand.
Before he knew what was happening, Shia's career, and really his life, took a very unpredictable turn. Mr. Thompson reached into the inside pocket of his jacket.

Mr. Thompson, or "Billy," as he liked to be called, kept his left hand on an object which Shia could not see, this turned out to be Billy's tape recorder; an object that Shia would see a lot of. Billy started talking and he was surprisingly calm.

After placing the tape recorder on Shia's desk,the new emplyee listened in shock as Mr. Thompson explained that he was tired of working with his arrogant brother, Carl Thompson, the Senior Partner. Shia was not at all prepared for this; he had hardly been an employee at the firm and he had a bad feeling.

Billy said "I have done a little background on you, Shia, and I like what I have learned. You and I are going to work together to get my trouble making brother out of this place; then you and I will make a lot of money.

The light haired man Jack looks down at his coffee, wondering what their next destination must be. He knows that Jane is not happy with their lodgings at the moment but they do not have many options. He has been avoiding telling her that they might have to continue driving until the next town. Their reception here has not been what he had hoped for. The two lane main street can be seen behind Jack through the diner window, the sun barely shining on a cloudy day.

Jane looks up at Jack and tries to figure out exactly what he is thinking. Her tank top has a thin red dragonfly shown on the front of her chest. This is a sign of their order, one that members use to communicate to each other. They have been hunted for so long that they have to be careful about how they communicate but symbols and art have been one method that they have used for years. Jane has not been happy with living on the road for so long and looks at Jack with disdain in her eyes. The motel that they stayed at last night can be seen through the windows behind her.

As Jack looks back at Jane, he spots danger walking through the diner door. He reaches into his leather jacket, ready to grab his weapon. He has been jumpy lately and does not want to overreact. If the other diners see that he has a weapon, it will not be good for their cover.

A dark haired man with a long nose walks up to the diner counter. The green and black register stands empty, the staff having walked into the back of the diner to get an order. The man wears a dark gray suit jacket with a black collared undershirt. He is trying to act natural but looks over at Jane and Jack's table. They are the two that he has been hunting and tracking for the last two weeks. Finally, he has caught up. Now is not the time to fight though, there are too many people around.

Jane looks up at Jack and spots the dark haired man as he walks up to the counter from the corner of her eye. Something does not feel right to her and she knows that they are going to be forced to make a decision, run or fight. She is so tired of running and living this life. Why wont they leave her alone?

At the height of the Old West, there stands a band of lawmen and women who do what must be done, even when they don't want to harm another. Jeff is one of them, his short gray hair and gray beard ust beneath his brown felt hat a testiment to his time spent upo this Earth. His eyes fixateed on the brown horse beneath him as he begins his dismount, the negative aspects of age showing with the pain on his face.

As he struggles to dismounnt his horse, showcases the lack of grace that comes with age, fellow lawman Matt and Lawwomen Hailee arrive, both looking on and marveling at the spectical before them, the dense forest of everygreen trees behind them in full view against a grassland that has seen better days.

As Jeff takes the reins of his horse, which seems reluctantly willing to couperate with him, his frame becomes engulfed by his heavy gray coat, a hint at the harsh cold, despite the prevelance of sunlight. Matt looks on, steadying his horse, as it attempts to react the the distress of Jeff's horse. Hailee, in her drark gray overcoat and gray felt hat, as still as her horse as she looks on at both of them.

Matt sits upon his horse, his face showing wringles and a brown mushstache which shows that he is far from young himself, his right hand resting on his horses reins and his left slumped at his side. A look of concer and unease on his face, the vastness of the wilderness behind him.

Despite the lack of grace from his dismount, Jeff is instilled with confidence. With thhe knowledge that they will win this day agaist the outlaws, just as the group had done many times before. His right had raising his pistol upwards and his eyes fixated on their target. The worries and frustrations of age leaving his face, being replaced with a steel reserve and determinatio that is unmatched.

Matt, despite his advanced age and his percieved experience, is still unsure of himself. Unsure of if he can still do what must be done. Looking at his own pistol closely as if to find some reason as to why he can't fight today, why he can't take another life. The mixture of emotions filling his mind as he reasons that he will save lives, while having to take another. Wondering if the life of a lawman in the Old West was his choice out of necessity, rather then true desire.

It had been a rough night for Emile. His drinking problem was becoming more serious, and, as he awoke from a long sleep, he realized with some embarrassment that he had slept on the floor the previous night.
Groggily, Emile reached his hand out in an attempt to get his bearings. His hand landed on a book that lay on a table.

Emile retrieved the book and flipped through it. It was the book his estranged wife had written. She had sent him an early copy, perhaps as a token of respect in spite of the difficulties their marriage now faced.
As he read the volume, Emile was hit with a flood of emotions. He still loved his wife.
Emile took a moment to engage in deep self-reflection. He knew he needed to get his life together.
Tom has a problem he needs to discuss with his superior.
He wants to know if Bill has a couple of moments to spare for a sit-down.
Bill doesn't respond. It seems he is engrossed in something else at the moment.
Tom's colleagues Terence and Christian tell Tom it's time to leave and urge him to go with them. Tom tells Bill he'll stop by later when Bill isn't so busy.
Bill tears himself away from his project, glares at Tom for a moment. Then sighs. He tells Tom he can spare a few minutes. Someone coughs timidly elsewhere in the room.

Bill whirls angrily toward a group of underlings, and screams at them to GET OUT IF THEY CAN'T KEEP QUIET! They scatter. Bill seethes, breathing hard. Tom stands wordlessly, wondering if he should've picked another day to ask for a raise.
